summary: Static code review and TypeScript compilation check for 'Export All Data' CSV feature in React Native/Expo app. Fixed TypeScript error (e.sentiment -> e.primaryEmotion). All implementation requirements verified correct.
critical_code_review_comments: export-data.ts correctly imports from all 6 required stores: journal-store, badges-store, user-stats-store, settings-store, onboarding-store, emotion-correction-store
CSV generation includes all 6 required sections: Journal Entries, User Stats, Badges, Settings, Emotion Corrections, Correction Summary
Export button has correct data-testid='export-data-button' at line 839 in settings.tsx
handleExportData handler (lines 213-223) correctly calls exportAllDataAsCsv with try/catch and loading state (isExporting)
Export button shows 'Exporting...' text while isExporting is true (line 866)
Export button is positioned above 'Reset All Data' button in Danger Zone section with divider separator
Uses expo-file-system for writing CSV and expo-sharing for sharing - correct Expo APIs
FIXED: TypeScript error - changed e.sentiment to e.primaryEmotion (sentiment property doesn't exist on JournalEntry type)

success_rate.backend: N/A - React Native app, no backend APIs
success_rate.frontend: 100% - All code review checks passed after fix

context_for_next_testing_agent: This is a React Native/Expo mobile app. No web frontend exists for Playwright testing. Testing was done via static code analysis and TypeScript compilation. The 'Export All Data' CSV feature implementation is complete and correct after fixing the TypeScript error.

settings_integration.position: Above Reset All Data button in Danger Zone
